item.text: Now I’ll patch in the adapted proof, replacing only the placeholder.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
path=Path('VoteProof_Liveness.tla')
s=path.read_text()
old='THEOREM Liveness == LiveSpec => C"'!LiveSpec'"\\nPROOF OBVIOUS\\n'
new=r'''THEOREM Liveness == LiveSpec => C"'!LiveSpec
<1> SUFFICES ASSUME NEW Q '"\\in Quorum, NEW b \\in Ballot
             PROVE  Spec /\\ LiveAssumption"'!(Q, b) => C!LiveSpec
  BY DEF LiveSpec, LiveAssumption

<1>a. IsFiniteSet(Q)
  BY QA, AcceptorFinite, SubsetOfFiniteSetFinite

<1>1. C!LiveSpec <=> C!Spec /'"\\ ([]<><<C"'!Next>>_C!vars '"\\/ []<>(chosen # {}))
  BY ValueNonempty, C"'!LiveSpecEquals

<1> DEFINE LNext == '"\\E self \\in Acceptor, c \\in Ballot :
                         /\\ BallotAction(self, c)
                         /\\ (self \\in Q) => (c =< b)

<1>2. Spec /\\ LiveAssumption"'!(Q, b) => [][LNext]_vars
  <2>1. /'"\\ TypeOK
        /\\ [Next]_vars
        /\\ [\\A self \\in Q : \\A c \\in Ballot : (c > b) => ~ BallotAction(self, c)]_vars
        => [LNext]_vars
    BY NextDef DEF LNext, Ballot
  <2>2. /\\ []TypeOK
        /\\ [][Next]_vars
        /\\ [][\\A self \\in Q : \\A c \\in Ballot : (c > b) => ~ BallotAction(self, c)]_vars
        => [][LNext]_vars
    BY <2>1, PTL
  <2>3. QED
    BY <2>2, VT2 DEF Spec, LiveAssumption, VInv

<1> DEFINE LNInv1 == \\A a \\in Q : maxBal[a] =< b
           LInv1  == VInv /\\ LNInv1

<1>3. LInv1 /\\ [LNext]_vars => LInv1'
  <2>1. SUFFICES ASSUME LInv1, [LNext]_vars
                 PROVE  LInv1'
    OBVIOUS
  <2>2. VInv'
    BY <2>1, NextDef, InductiveInvariance DEF LInv1, VInv
  <2>3. LNInv1'
    BY <2>1, QA DEF BallotAction, IncreaseMaxBal, VoteFor, VInv, TypeOK, vars
  <2>4. QED
    BY <2>2, <2>3

<1>4. \\A a \\in Q :
          VInv /\\ (maxBal[a] = b) /\\ [LNext]_vars => VInv' /\\ (maxBal'[a] = b)
  <2>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW a \\in Q,
                        VInv, maxBal[a] = b, [LNext]_vars
                 PROVE  VInv' /\\ (maxBal'[a] = b)
    OBVIOUS
  <2>2. VInv'
    BY <2>1, NextDef, InductiveInvariance DEF VInv
  <2>3. maxBal'[a] = b
    BY <2>1, QA DEF BallotAction, IncreaseMaxBal, VoteFor, VInv, TypeOK, Ballot, vars
  <2>4. QED
    BY <2>2, <2>3

<1>5. Spec /\\ LiveAssumption"'!(Q, b) =>
         <>[]('"\\A self \\in Q : maxBal[self] = b)
  <2>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW self \\in Q
                 PROVE  Spec /\\ LiveAssumption"'!(Q, b) => <>[](maxBal[self] = b)
    BY <1>a, EventuallyAlwaysForall
  <2> DEFINE P ==  LInv1 /'"\\ ~(maxBal[self] = b)
             QQ == LInv1 /\\ (maxBal[self] = b)
             A == BallotAction(self, b)
  <2>2. [][LNext]_vars /\\ WF_vars(A) => (LInv1 ~> QQ)
    <3>1. P /\\ [LNext]_vars => (P' \\/ QQ')
      BY <1>3
    <3>2. P /\\ <<LNext /\\ A>>_vars => QQ'
      <4>1. SUFFICES ASSUME LInv1, LNext, A
                     PROVE  QQ'
        OBVIOUS
      <4>2. LInv1'
        BY <4>1, <1>3
      <4>3. CASE IncreaseMaxBal(self, b)
        BY <4>1, <4>2, <4>3, QA DEF IncreaseMaxBal, VInv, TypeOK
      <4>4. CASE \\E v \\in Value : VoteFor(self, b, v)
        BY <4>1, <4>2, <4>4, QA DEF VoteFor, VInv, TypeOK
      <4>5. QED
        BY <4>1, <4>3, <4>4 DEF BallotAction
    <3>3. P => ENABLED <<A>>_vars
      <4>1. (ENABLED <<A>>_vars) <=>
              \\E votesp, maxBalp:
                 /\\ \\/ /\\ b > maxBal[self]
                       /\\ maxBalp = [maxBal EXCEPT "'![self] = b]
                       /'"\\ votesp = votes
                    \\/ \\E v \\in Value :
                          /\\ maxBal[self] \\leq b
                          /\\ DidNotVoteIn(self, b)
                          /\\ \\A p \\in Acceptor \\ {self} :
                                \\A w \\in Value : VotedFor(p, b, w) => (w = v)
                          /\\ SafeAt(b, v)
                          /\\ votesp = [votes EXCEPT "'![self] = votes[self]
                                                                '"\\cup {<<b, v>>}]
                          /\\ maxBalp = [maxBal EXCEPT "'![self] = b]
                 /'"\\ <<votesp, maxBalp>> # <<votes, maxBal>>
        BY DEF BallotAction, IncreaseMaxBal, VoteFor, vars
      <4>2. SUFFICES ASSUME P
                    PROVE \\E votesp, maxBalp:
                              /\\ b > maxBal[self]
                              /\\ maxBalp = [maxBal EXCEPT "'![self] = b]
                              /'"\\ votesp = votes
                              /\\ <<votesp, maxBalp>> # <<votes, maxBal>>
        BY <4>1
      <4>3. WITNESS votes, [maxBal EXCEPT "'![self] = b]
      <4>4. QED
        BY QA DEF VInv, TypeOK, Ballot
    <3>4. QED
      BY <3>1, <3>2, <3>3, PTL
  <2>3. QQ /'"\\ [][LNext]_vars => []QQ
    <3>1. QQ /\\ [LNext]_vars => QQ'
      BY <1>3, <1>4
    <3>2. QED
      BY <3>1, PTL
  <2>4. []QQ => [](maxBal[self] = b)
    BY PTL
  <2>5. LiveAssumption"'!(Q,b) => WF_vars(A)
    BY DEF LiveAssumption
  <2>6. Spec => LInv1
    <3>1. Init => VInv
      BY InitImpliesInv
    <3>2. Init => LNInv1
      BY QA DEF Init, Ballot
    <3>3. QED
      BY <3>1, <3>2 DEF Spec
  <2>7. QED
    BY <2>2, <2>3, <2>4, <2>5, <2>6, <1>2, PTL

<1> DEFINE LNInv2 == '"\\A a \\in Q : maxBal[a] = b
           LInv2  == VInv /\\ LNInv2

<1>6. LInv2 /\\ [LNext]_vars => LInv2'
  BY <1>4, QuorumNonEmpty

<1>7. Spec /\\ LiveAssumption"'!(Q, b) => <>[](chosen # {})
  <2> DEFINE Voted(a) == '"\\E v \\in Value : VotedFor(a, b, v)
  <2>1. Spec /\\ LiveAssumption"'!(Q, b) => <>[]LInv2
    <3>1. Spec /'"\\ LiveAssumption"'!(Q,b) => <>[]LNInv2
      BY <1>5
    <3>2. QED
      BY <3>1, VT2, PTL DEF LInv2, VInv
  <2>2. LInv2 /'"\\ (\\A a \\in Q : Voted(a)) => (chosen # {})
    <3>1. SUFFICES ASSUME LInv2,
                          \\A a \\in Q : Voted(a)
                   PROVE  chosen # {}
      OBVIOUS
    <3>2. \\E v \\in Value : \\A a \\in Q :  VotedFor(a, b, v)
      <4>1. PICK a0 \\in Q, v \\in Value : VotedFor(a0, b, v)
        BY <3>1, QuorumNonEmpty
      <4>2. ASSUME NEW a \\in Q
            PROVE  VotedFor(a, b, v)
        BY <3>1, <4>1, QA DEF VInv, VInv3
      <4>3. QED
        BY <4>2
    <3>3. QED
      BY <3>2 DEF chosen, ChosenIn
  <2>3. Spec /\\ LiveAssumption"'!(Q, b) => ('"\\A a \\in Q : <>[]Voted(a))
    <3>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW self \\in Q
                   PROVE  Spec /\\ LiveAssumption"'!(Q, b) => <>[]Voted(self)
      OBVIOUS
    <3>2. Spec /'"\\ LiveAssumption"'!(Q, b) => <>Voted(self)
      <4>1. [][LNext]_vars /'"\\ WF_vars(BallotAction(self, b))
               => ((LInv2 /\\ ~Voted(self)) ~> LInv2 /\\ Voted(self))
        <5> DEFINE P == LInv2 /\\ ~Voted(self)
                   QQ == LInv2 /\\ Voted(self)
                   A == BallotAction(self, b)
        <5>1. P /\\ [LNext]_vars => (P' \\/ QQ')
          BY <1>6
        <5>2. P /\\ <<LNext /\\ A>>_vars => QQ'
          <6>1. SUFFICES ASSUME P,
                                LNext,
                                A
                         PROVE QQ'
            OBVIOUS
          <6>2. CASE \\E v \\in Value : VoteFor(self, b, v)
            BY <6>1, <6>2, <5>1, QA DEF VoteFor, Voted, VotedFor, LInv2, VInv, TypeOK
          <6>3. CASE IncreaseMaxBal(self, b)
            BY <6>1, <6>3 DEF IncreaseMaxBal, Ballot
          <6>4. QED
            BY <6>1, <6>2, <6>3 DEF BallotAction
        <5>3. P => ENABLED <<A>>_vars
          <6>1. SUFFICES ASSUME P
                         PROVE  ENABLED <<A>>_vars
            OBVIOUS
          <6>2. (ENABLED <<A>>_vars) <=>
                  \\E votesp, maxBalp :
                     /\\ \\/ /\\ b > maxBal[self]
                           /\\ maxBalp = [maxBal EXCEPT "'![self] = b]
                           /'"\\ votesp = votes
                        \\/ \\E v \\in Value :
                              /\\ maxBal[self] \\leq b
                              /\\ DidNotVoteIn(self, b)
                              /\\ \\A p \\in Acceptor \\ {self} :
                                  \\A w \\in Value : VotedFor(p, b, w) => (w = v)
                              /\\ SafeAt(b, v)
                              /\\ votesp = [votes EXCEPT "'![self] = votes[self]
                                                                '"\\cup {<<b, v>>}]
                              /\\ maxBalp = [maxBal EXCEPT "'![self] = b]
                     /'"\\ <<votesp, maxBalp>> #  <<votes, maxBal>>
            BY DEF BallotAction, IncreaseMaxBal, VoteFor, vars
          <6>3. SUFFICES
                  \\E votesp, maxBalp:
                     /\\ \\E v \\in Value :
                           /\\ maxBal[self] \\leq b
                           /\\ DidNotVoteIn(self, b)
                           /\\ \\A p \\in Acceptor \\ {self} :
                                \\A w \\in Value : VotedFor(p, b, w) => (w = v)
                           /\\ SafeAt(b, v)
                           /\\ votesp = [votes EXCEPT "'![self] = votes[self]
                                                                '"\\cup {<<b, v>>}]
                           /\\ maxBalp = [maxBal EXCEPT "'![self] = b]
                     /'"\\ <<votesp, maxBalp>> #  <<votes, maxBal>>
            BY <6>2
          <6> DEFINE someVoted == \\E p \\in Acceptor \\ {self} :
                                   \\E w \\in Value : VotedFor(p, b, w)
                     vp == CHOOSE p \\in Acceptor \\ {self} :
                                   \\E w \\in Value : VotedFor(p, b, w)
                     vpval == CHOOSE w \\in Value : VotedFor(vp, b, w)
          <6>4. someVoted => /\\ vp \\in Acceptor
                             /\\ vpval \\in Value
                             /\\ VotedFor(vp, b, vpval)
            BY Zenon
          <6> DEFINE v == IF someVoted THEN vpval
                                       ELSE CHOOSE v \\in Value : SafeAt(b, v)
          <6>5. (v \\in Value) /\\ SafeAt(b, v)
            BY <6>1, <6>4, VT4 DEF VInv, VInv2, Ballot, LInv2
          <6> DEFINE votesp == [votes EXCEPT "'![self] = votes[self] '"\\cup {<<b, v>>}]
                     maxBalp == [maxBal EXCEPT "'![self] = b]
          <6>6. WITNESS votesp, maxBalp
          <6>7. SUFFICES /'"\\ maxBal[self] \\leq b
                       /\\ DidNotVoteIn(self, b)
                       /\\ \\A p \\in Acceptor \\ {self} :
                                \\A w \\in Value : VotedFor(p, b, w) => (w = v)
                       /\\ votesp # votes
            BY <6>5, Zenon
          <6>8. maxBal[self] \\leq b
            BY <6>1 DEF Ballot, LInv2
          <6>9. DidNotVoteIn(self, b)
            BY <6>1 DEF Voted, DidNotVoteIn
          <6>10. ASSUME NEW p \\in Acceptor \\ {self},
                       NEW w \\in Value,
                       VotedFor(p, b, w)
                PROVE  w = v
            BY <6>10, <6>4, <6>1 DEF VInv, VInv3, LInv2
          <6>11. votesp # votes
            <7>1. votesp[self] = votes[self] \\cup {<<b, v>>}
              BY <6>1, QA DEF LInv2, VInv, TypeOK
            <7>2. \\A w \\in Value : <<b, w>> \\notin votes[self]
              BY <6>9 DEF DidNotVoteIn, VotedFor
            <7>3. QED
              BY <7>1, <7>2, <6>5, Zenon
          <6>12. QED
            BY <6>8, <6>9, <6>10, <6>11, Zenon
        <5>4. QED
          BY <5>1, <5>2, <5>3, PTL
      <4>2. []LInv2 /\\ ((LInv2 /\\ ~Voted(self)) ~> LInv2 /\\ Voted(self))
              => <>Voted(self)
        BY PTL
      <4>3. LiveAssumption"'!(Q, b) => WF_vars(BallotAction(self,b))
        BY DEF LiveAssumption
      <4>4. QED
        BY <1>2, <2>1, <4>1, <4>2, <4>3, PTL
    <3>3. Spec => [](Voted(self) => []Voted(self))
      <4>1. (VInv /'"\\ Voted(self)) /\\ [Next]_vars => (VInv /\\ Voted(self))'
        <5>1. SUFFICES ASSUME VInv, Voted(self), [Next]_vars
                     PROVE  VInv' /\\ Voted(self)'
          OBVIOUS
        <5>2. VInv'
          BY InductiveInvariance
        <5>3. Voted(self)'
          <6>1. CASE vars' = vars
            BY DEF vars, Voted, VotedFor
          <6>2. CASE Next
            <7>1. PICK a \\in Acceptor, c \\in Ballot : BallotAction(a, c)
              BY NextDef DEF VInv
            <7>2. CASE IncreaseMaxBal(a, c)
              BY <7>2 DEF IncreaseMaxBal, Voted, VotedFor
            <7>3. CASE \\E v \\in Value : VoteFor(a, c, v)
              BY <7>3, QA DEF VInv, TypeOK, VoteFor, Voted, VotedFor
            <7>4. QED
              BY <7>1, <7>2, <7>3 DEF BallotAction
          <6>3. QED
            OBVIOUS
        <5>4. QED
          BY <5>2, <5>3
      <4>2. QED
        BY <4>1, VT2, PTL DEF Spec
    <3>4. QED
      BY <3>2, <3>3, PTL
  <2>4. (\\A a \\in Q : <>[]Voted(a)) => <>[](\\A a \\in Q : Voted(a))
    BY <1>a, EventuallyAlwaysForall
  <2>5. QED
    BY <2>1, VT2, <2>2, <2>3, <2>4, PTL

<1>8. QED
  <2>1. Spec /\\ LiveAssumption"'!(Q, b) => C!Spec /'"\\ <>[](chosen # {})
    BY VT3, <1>7
  <2>2. Spec /\\ LiveAssumption"'!(Q, b) => C!Spec /'"\\ []<>(chosen # {})
    BY <2>1, PTL
  <2>3. QED
    BY <2>2, <1>1
'''

    raise SystemExit('placeholder not found')

/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
path=Path('VoteProof_Liveness.tla')
s=path.read_text()
old='THEOREM Liveness == LiveSpec => C"'!LiveSpec'"\\nPROOF OBVIOUS\\n'
new=r'''THEOREM Liveness == LiveSpec => C"'!LiveSpec
<1> SUFFICES ASSUME NEW Q '"\\in Quorum, NEW b \\in Ballot
             PROVE  Spec /\\ LiveAssumption"'!(Q, b) => C!LiveSpec
  BY DEF LiveSpec, LiveAssumption

<1>a. IsFiniteSet(Q)
  BY QA, AcceptorFinite, SubsetOfFiniteSetFinite

<1>1. C!LiveSpec <=> C!Spec /'"\\ ([]<><<C"'!Next>>_C!vars '"\\/ []<>(chosen # {}))
  BY ValueNonempty, C"'!LiveSpecEquals

<1> DEFINE LNext == '"\\E self \\in Acceptor, c \\in Ballot :
                         /\\ BallotAction(self, c)
                         /\\ (self \\in Q) => (c =< b)

<1>2. Spec /\\ LiveAssumption"'!(Q, b) => [][LNext]_vars
  <2>1. /'"\\ TypeOK
        /\\ [Next]_vars
        /\\ [\\A self \\in Q : \\A c \\in Ballot : (c > b) => ~ BallotAction(self, c)]_vars
        => [LNext]_vars
    BY NextDef DEF LNext, Ballot
  <2>2. /\\ []TypeOK
        /\\ [][Next]_vars
        /\\ [][\\A self \\in Q : \\A c \\in Ballot : (c > b) => ~ BallotAction(self, c)]_vars
        => [][LNext]_vars
    BY <2>1, PTL
  <2>3. QED
    BY <2>2, VT2 DEF Spec, LiveAssumption, VInv

<1> DEFINE LNInv1 == \\A a \\in Q : maxBal[a] =< b
           LInv1  == VInv /\\ LNInv1

<1>3. LInv1 /\\ [LNext]_vars => LInv1'
  <2>1. SUFFICES ASSUME LInv1, [LNext]_vars
                 PROVE  LInv1'
    OBVIOUS
  <2>2. VInv'
    BY <2>1, NextDef, InductiveInvariance DEF LInv1, VInv
  <2>3. LNInv1'
    BY <2>1, QA DEF BallotAction, IncreaseMaxBal, VoteFor, VInv, TypeOK, vars
  <2>4. QED
    BY <2>2, <2>3

<1>4. \\A a \\in Q :
          VInv /\\ (maxBal[a] = b) /\\ [LNext]_vars => VInv' /\\ (maxBal'[a] = b)
  <2>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW a \\in Q,
                        VInv, maxBal[a] = b, [LNext]_vars
                 PROVE  VInv' /\\ (maxBal'[a] = b)
    OBVIOUS
  <2>2. VInv'
    BY <2>1, NextDef, InductiveInvariance DEF VInv
  <2>3. maxBal'[a] = b
    BY <2>1, QA DEF BallotAction, IncreaseMaxBal, VoteFor, VInv, TypeOK, Ballot, vars
  <2>4. QED
    BY <2>2, <2>3

<1>5. Spec /\\ LiveAssumption"'!(Q, b) =>
         <>[]('"\\A self \\in Q : maxBal[self] = b)
  <2>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW self \\in Q
                 PROVE  Spec /\\ LiveAssumption"'!(Q, b) => <>[](maxBal[self] = b)
    BY <1>a, EventuallyAlwaysForall
  <2> DEFINE P ==  LInv1 /'"\\ ~(maxBal[self] = b)
             QQ == LInv1 /\\ (maxBal[self] = b)
             A == BallotAction(self, b)
  <2>2. [][LNext]_vars /\\ WF_vars(A) => (LInv1 ~> QQ)
    <3>1. P /\\ [LNext]_vars => (P' \\/ QQ')
      BY <1>3
    <3>2. P /\\ <<LNext /\\ A>>_vars => QQ'
      <4>1. SUFFICES ASSUME LInv1, LNext, A
                     PROVE  QQ'
        OBVIOUS
      <4>2. LInv1'
        BY <4>1, <1>3
      <4>3. CASE IncreaseMaxBal(self, b)
        BY <4>1, <4>2, <4>3, QA DEF IncreaseMaxBal, VInv, TypeOK
      <4>4. CASE \\E v \\in Value : VoteFor(self, b, v)
        BY <4>1, <4>2, <4>4, QA DEF VoteFor, VInv, TypeOK
      <4>5. QED
        BY <4>1, <4>3, <4>4 DEF BallotAction
    <3>3. P => ENABLED <<A>>_vars
      <4>1. (ENABLED <<A>>_vars) <=>
              \\E votesp, maxBalp:
                 /\\ \\/ /\\ b > maxBal[self]
                       /\\ maxBalp = [maxBal EXCEPT "'![self] = b]
                       /'"\\ votesp = votes
                    \\/ \\E v \\in Value :
                          /\\ maxBal[self] \\leq b
                          /\\ DidNotVoteIn(self, b)
                          /\\ \\A p \\in Acceptor \\ {self} :
                                \\A w \\in Value : VotedFor(p, b, w) => (w = v)
                          /\\ SafeAt(b, v)
                          /\\ votesp = [votes EXCEPT "'![self] = votes[self]
                                                                '"\\cup {<<b, v>>}]
                          /\\ maxBalp = [maxBal EXCEPT "'![self] = b]
                 /'"\\ <<votesp, maxBalp>> # <<votes, maxBal>>
        BY DEF BallotAction, IncreaseMaxBal, VoteFor, vars
      <4>2. SUFFICES ASSUME P
                    PROVE \\E votesp, maxBalp:
                              /\\ b > maxBal[self]
                              /\\ maxBalp = [maxBal EXCEPT "'![self] = b]
                              /'"\\ votesp = votes
                              /\\ <<votesp, maxBalp>> # <<votes, maxBal>>
        BY <4>1
      <4>3. WITNESS votes, [maxBal EXCEPT "'![self] = b]
      <4>4. QED
        BY QA DEF VInv, TypeOK, Ballot
    <3>4. QED
      BY <3>1, <3>2, <3>3, PTL
  <2>3. QQ /'"\\ [][LNext]_vars => []QQ
    <3>1. QQ /\\ [LNext]_vars => QQ'
      BY <1>3, <1>4
    <3>2. QED
      BY <3>1, PTL
  <2>4. []QQ => [](maxBal[self] = b)
    BY PTL
  <2>5. LiveAssumption"'!(Q,b) => WF_vars(A)
    BY DEF LiveAssumption
  <2>6. Spec => LInv1
    <3>1. Init => VInv
      BY InitImpliesInv
    <3>2. Init => LNInv1
      BY QA DEF Init, Ballot
    <3>3. QED
      BY <3>1, <3>2 DEF Spec
  <2>7. QED
    BY <2>2, <2>3, <2>4, <2>5, <2>6, <1>2, PTL

<1> DEFINE LNInv2 == '"\\A a \\in Q : maxBal[a] = b
           LInv2  == VInv /\\ LNInv2

<1>6. LInv2 /\\ [LNext]_vars => LInv2'
  BY <1>4, QuorumNonEmpty

<1>7. Spec /\\ LiveAssumption"'!(Q, b) => <>[](chosen # {})
  <2> DEFINE Voted(a) == '"\\E v \\in Value : VotedFor(a, b, v)
  <2>1. Spec /\\ LiveAssumption"'!(Q, b) => <>[]LInv2
    <3>1. Spec /'"\\ LiveAssumption"'!(Q,b) => <>[]LNInv2
      BY <1>5
    <3>2. QED
      BY <3>1, VT2, PTL DEF LInv2, VInv
  <2>2. LInv2 /'"\\ (\\A a \\in Q : Voted(a)) => (chosen # {})
    <3>1. SUFFICES ASSUME LInv2,
                          \\A a \\in Q : Voted(a)
                   PROVE  chosen # {}
      OBVIOUS
    <3>2. \\E v \\in Value : \\A a \\in Q :  VotedFor(a, b, v)
      <4>1. PICK a0 \\in Q, v \\in Value : VotedFor(a0, b, v)
        BY <3>1, QuorumNonEmpty
      <4>2. ASSUME NEW a \\in Q
            PROVE  VotedFor(a, b, v)
        BY <3>1, <4>1, QA DEF VInv, VInv3
      <4>3. QED
        BY <4>2
    <3>3. QED
      BY <3>2 DEF chosen, ChosenIn
  <2>3. Spec /\\ LiveAssumption"'!(Q, b) => ('"\\A a \\in Q : <>[]Voted(a))
    <3>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW self \\in Q
                   PROVE  Spec /\\ LiveAssumption"'!(Q, b) => <>[]Voted(self)
      OBVIOUS
    <3>2. Spec /'"\\ LiveAssumption"'!(Q, b) => <>Voted(self)
      <4>1. [][LNext]_vars /'"\\ WF_vars(BallotAction(self, b))
               => ((LInv2 /\\ ~Voted(self)) ~> LInv2 /\\ Voted(self))
        <5> DEFINE P == LInv2 /\\ ~Voted(self)
                   QQ == LInv2 /\\ Voted(self)
                   A == BallotAction(self, b)
        <5>1. P /\\ [LNext]_vars => (P' \\/ QQ')
          BY <1>6
        <5>2. P /\\ <<LNext /\\ A>>_vars => QQ'
          <6>1. SUFFICES ASSUME P,
                                LNext,
                                A
                         PROVE QQ'
            OBVIOUS
          <6>2. CASE \\E v \\in Value : VoteFor(self, b, v)
            BY <6>1, <6>2, <5>1, QA DEF VoteFor, Voted, VotedFor, LInv2, VInv, TypeOK
          <6>3. CASE IncreaseMaxBal(self, b)
            BY <6>1, <6>3 DEF IncreaseMaxBal, Ballot
          <6>4. QED
            BY <6>1, <6>2, <6>3 DEF BallotAction
        <5>3. P => ENABLED <<A>>_vars
          <6>1. SUFFICES ASSUME P
                         PROVE  ENABLED <<A>>_vars
            OBVIOUS
          <6>2. (ENABLED <<A>>_vars) <=>
                  \\E votesp, maxBalp :
                     /\\ \\/ /\\ b > maxBal[self]
                           /\\ maxBalp = [maxBal EXCEPT "'![self] = b]
                           /'"\\ votesp = votes
                        \\/ \\E v \\in Value :
                              /\\ maxBal[self] \\leq b
                              /\\ DidNotVoteIn(self, b)
                              /\\ \\A p \\in Acceptor \\ {self} :
                                  \\A w \\in Value : VotedFor(p, b, w) => (w = v)
                              /\\ SafeAt(b, v)
                              /\\ votesp = [votes EXCEPT "'![self] = votes[self]
                                                                '"\\cup {<<b, v>>}]
                              /\\ maxBalp = [maxBal EXCEPT "'![self] = b]
                     /'"\\ <<votesp, maxBalp>> #  <<votes, maxBal>>
            BY DEF BallotAction, IncreaseMaxBal, VoteFor, vars
          <6>3. SUFFICES
                  \\E votesp, maxBalp:
                     /\\ \\E v \\in Value :
                           /\\ maxBal[self] \\leq b
                           /\\ DidNotVoteIn(self, b)
                           /\\ \\A p \\in Acceptor \\ {self} :
                                \\A w \\in Value : VotedFor(p, b, w) => (w = v)
                           /\\ SafeAt(b, v)
                           /\\ votesp = [votes EXCEPT "'![self] = votes[self]
                                                                '"\\cup {<<b, v>>}]
                           /\\ maxBalp = [maxBal EXCEPT "'![self] = b]
                     /'"\\ <<votesp, maxBalp>> #  <<votes, maxBal>>
            BY <6>2
          <6> DEFINE someVoted == \\E p \\in Acceptor \\ {self} :
                                   \\E w \\in Value : VotedFor(p, b, w)
                     vp == CHOOSE p \\in Acceptor \\ {self} :
                                   \\E w \\in Value : VotedFor(p, b, w)
                     vpval == CHOOSE w \\in Value : VotedFor(vp, b, w)
          <6>4. someVoted => /\\ vp \\in Acceptor
                             /\\ vpval \\in Value
                             /\\ VotedFor(vp, b, vpval)
            BY Zenon
          <6> DEFINE v == IF someVoted THEN vpval
                                       ELSE CHOOSE v \\in Value : SafeAt(b, v)
          <6>5. (v \\in Value) /\\ SafeAt(b, v)
            BY <6>1, <6>4, VT4 DEF VInv, VInv2, Ballot, LInv2
          <6> DEFINE votesp == [votes EXCEPT "'![self] = votes[self] '"\\cup {<<b, v>>}]
                     maxBalp == [maxBal EXCEPT "'![self] = b]
          <6>6. WITNESS votesp, maxBalp
          <6>7. SUFFICES /'"\\ maxBal[self] \\leq b
                       /\\ DidNotVoteIn(self, b)
                       /\\ \\A p \\in Acceptor \\ {self} :
                                \\A w \\in Value : VotedFor(p, b, w) => (w = v)
                       /\\ votesp # votes
            BY <6>5, Zenon
          <6>8. maxBal[self] \\leq b
            BY <6>1 DEF Ballot, LInv2
          <6>9. DidNotVoteIn(self, b)
            BY <6>1 DEF Voted, DidNotVoteIn
          <6>10. ASSUME NEW p \\in Acceptor \\ {self},
                       NEW w \\in Value,
                       VotedFor(p, b, w)
                PROVE  w = v
            BY <6>10, <6>4, <6>1 DEF VInv, VInv3, LInv2
          <6>11. votesp # votes
            <7>1. votesp[self] = votes[self] \\cup {<<b, v>>}
              BY <6>1, QA DEF LInv2, VInv, TypeOK
            <7>2. \\A w \\in Value : <<b, w>> \\notin votes[self]
              BY <6>9 DEF DidNotVoteIn, VotedFor
            <7>3. QED
              BY <7>1, <7>2, <6>5, Zenon
          <6>12. QED
            BY <6>8, <6>9, <6>10, <6>11, Zenon
        <5>4. QED
          BY <5>1, <5>2, <5>3, PTL
      <4>2. []LInv2 /\\ ((LInv2 /\\ ~Voted(self)) ~> LInv2 /\\ Voted(self))
              => <>Voted(self)
        BY PTL
      <4>3. LiveAssumption"'!(Q, b) => WF_vars(BallotAction(self,b))
        BY DEF LiveAssumption
      <4>4. QED
        BY <1>2, <2>1, <4>1, <4>2, <4>3, PTL
    <3>3. Spec => [](Voted(self) => []Voted(self))
      <4>1. (VInv /'"\\ Voted(self)) /\\ [Next]_vars => (VInv /\\ Voted(self))'
        <5>1. SUFFICES ASSUME VInv, Voted(self), [Next]_vars
                     PROVE  VInv' /\\ Voted(self)'
          OBVIOUS
        <5>2. VInv'
          BY InductiveInvariance
        <5>3. Voted(self)'
          <6>1. CASE vars' = vars
            BY DEF vars, Voted, VotedFor
          <6>2. CASE Next
            <7>1. PICK a \\in Acceptor, c \\in Ballot : BallotAction(a, c)
              BY NextDef DEF VInv
            <7>2. CASE IncreaseMaxBal(a, c)
              BY <7>2 DEF IncreaseMaxBal, Voted, VotedFor
            <7>3. CASE \\E v \\in Value : VoteFor(a, c, v)
              BY <7>3, QA DEF VInv, TypeOK, VoteFor, Voted, VotedFor
            <7>4. QED
              BY <7>1, <7>2, <7>3 DEF BallotAction
          <6>3. QED
            OBVIOUS
        <5>4. QED
          BY <5>2, <5>3
      <4>2. QED
        BY <4>1, VT2, PTL DEF Spec
    <3>4. QED
      BY <3>2, <3>3, PTL
  <2>4. (\\A a \\in Q : <>[]Voted(a)) => <>[](\\A a \\in Q : Voted(a))
    BY <1>a, EventuallyAlwaysForall
  <2>5. QED
    BY <2>1, VT2, <2>2, <2>3, <2>4, PTL

<1>8. QED
  <2>1. Spec /\\ LiveAssumption"'!(Q, b) => C!Spec /'"\\ <>[](chosen # {})
    BY VT3, <1>7
  <2>2. Spec /\\ LiveAssumption"'!(Q, b) => C!Spec /'"\\ []<>(chosen # {})
    BY <2>1, PTL
  <2>3. QED
    BY <2>2, <1>1
'''
if old not in s:
    raise SystemExit('placeholder not found')

from pathlib import Path
p=Path('VoteProof_Liveness.tla')
s=p.read_text()
s=s.replace('BY <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall\\n  <2> DEFINE P ==  LInv1', 'BY <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall"'!(Q)'"\\n  <2> DEFINE P ==  LInv1')
s=s.replace('BY <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall\\n  <2>5. QED', 'BY <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall"'!(Q)'"\\n  <2>5. QED')
p.write_text(s)
PY

item.text: I’ll test `USE ... WITH` variants in a scratch copy to find TLAPS’s accepted higher-order instantiation form.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "for repl in \\
'USE EventuallyAlwaysForall WITH S <- Q, P <- (LAMBDA x : maxBal[x] = b)\\n    BY <1>3' \\
'USE EventuallyAlwaysForall WITH S <- Q, P(x) <- maxBal[x] = b\\n    BY <1>3' \\
'USE EventuallyAlwaysForall WITH S <- Q, P(_) <- maxBal[_] = b\\n    BY <1>3' \\
'BY <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall WITH S <- Q, P(x) <- maxBal[x] = b' \\
'BY <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall WITH S <- Q, P(_) <- maxBal[_] = b' \\
; do
  cp /tmp/VPL.bak VoteProof_Liveness.tla
  python3 - <<PY
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('VoteProof_Liveness.tla')
s=p.read_text()
s=s.replace('BY <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall\\n  <2> DEFINE P ==  LInv1', '''"'$repl'"\\n  <2> DEFINE P ==  LInv1''')
p.write_text(s)
PY
  echo '--- testing:' \""'$repl"
  ~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps --line 870 VoteProof_Liveness.tla 2>&1 | head -n 35
  echo rc:${PIPESTATUS[0]}
done
cp /tmp/VPL.bak VoteProof_Liveness.tla'

/bin/zsh -lc "for repl in \\
'USE EventuallyAlwaysForall WITH S <- Q, P <- (LAMBDA x : maxBal[x] = b)\\n    BY <1>3' \\
'USE EventuallyAlwaysForall WITH S <- Q, P(x) <- maxBal[x] = b\\n    BY <1>3' \\
'USE EventuallyAlwaysForall WITH S <- Q, P(_) <- maxBal[_] = b\\n    BY <1>3' \\
'BY <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall WITH S <- Q, P(x) <- maxBal[x] = b' \\
'BY <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall WITH S <- Q, P(_) <- maxBal[_] = b' \\
; do
  cp /tmp/VPL.bak VoteProof_Liveness.tla
  python3 - <<PY
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('VoteProof_Liveness.tla')
s=p.read_text()
s=s.replace('BY <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall\\n  <2> DEFINE P ==  LInv1', '''"'$repl'"\\n  <2> DEFINE P ==  LInv1''')
p.write_text(s)
PY
  echo '--- testing:' \""'$repl"
  ~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps --line 870 VoteProof_Liveness.tla 2>&1 | head -n 35
  echo rc:${PIPESTATUS[0]}
done
cp /tmp/VPL.bak VoteProof_Liveness.tla'

item.text: I’ll test a named local temporal operator for the finite-forall theorem; TLAPS often infers `NEW P(_)` better from a named operator than a raw expression.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "cp VoteProof_Liveness.tla /tmp/VPL.bak2 && apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: VoteProof_Liveness.tla
@@
 <1>7. Spec /\\ LiveAssumptionFor(Q, b) => <>[](\\A self \\in Q : maxBal[self] = b)
+  <2> DEFINE MBIsB(x) == maxBal[x] = b
   <2>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW self \\in Q
                  PROVE  Spec /\\ LiveAssumptionFor(Q, b) => <>[](maxBal[self] = b)
-    BY <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall
+    BY <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall DEF MBIsB
*** End Patch
PATCH
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps --line 871 VoteProof_Liveness.tla ; rc="'$?; cp /tmp/VPL.bak2 VoteProof_Liveness.tla; exit $rc'

/bin/zsh -lc "cp VoteProof_Liveness.tla /tmp/VPL.bak2 && apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: VoteProof_Liveness.tla
@@
 <1>7. Spec /\\ LiveAssumptionFor(Q, b) => <>[](\\A self \\in Q : maxBal[self] = b)
+  <2> DEFINE MBIsB(x) == maxBal[x] = b
   <2>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW self \\in Q
                  PROVE  Spec /\\ LiveAssumptionFor(Q, b) => <>[](maxBal[self] = b)
-    BY <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall
+    BY <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall DEF MBIsB
*** End Patch
PATCH
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps --line 871 VoteProof_Liveness.tla ; rc="'$?; cp /tmp/VPL.bak2 VoteProof_Liveness.tla; exit $rc'

item.text: I found two structural issues: an overly strong global assumption and temporal-forall application under an implication. I’ll rewrite those blocks into explicit assumption subproofs.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: VoteProof_Liveness.tla
@@
 <1>4. Spec /\\ LiveAssumptionFor(Q, b) => [][LNext]_vars
   <2>1. /\\ TypeOK
         /\\ [Next]_vars
         /\\ [\\A self \\in Q : \\A c \\in Ballot : (c > b) => ~ BallotAction(self, c)]_vars
         => [LNext]_vars
     BY NextDef DEF LNext, Ballot, LiveAssumptionFor
   <2>2. /\\ []TypeOK
         /\\ [][Next]_vars
         /\\ [][\\A self \\in Q : \\A c \\in Ballot : (c > b) => ~ BallotAction(self, c)]_vars
         => [][LNext]_vars
     BY <2>1, PTL
-  <2>3. QED
-    BY <2>2, VT2 DEF Spec, VInv, LiveAssumptionFor
+  <2>3. LiveAssumptionFor(Q, b)
+          => [][\\A self \\in Q : \\A c \\in Ballot : (c > b) => ~ BallotAction(self, c)]_vars
+    BY AlwaysForall DEF LiveAssumptionFor
+  <2>4. QED
+    BY <2>2, <2>3, VT2 DEF Spec, VInv
@@
 <1>7. Spec /\\ LiveAssumptionFor(Q, b) => <>[](\\A self \\in Q : maxBal[self] = b)
-  <2>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW self \\in Q
-                 PROVE  Spec /\\ LiveAssumptionFor(Q, b) => <>[](maxBal[self] = b)
-    BY <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall
+  <2>1. SUFFICES ASSUME Spec /\\ LiveAssumptionFor(Q, b)
+                 PROVE  <>[](\\A self \\in Q : maxBal[self] = b)
+    OBVIOUS
+  <2>2. \\A self \\in Q : <>[](maxBal[self] = b)
+  <3>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW self \\in Q
+                 PROVE  <>[](maxBal[self] = b)
+    OBVIOUS
   <2> DEFINE P ==  LInv1 /\\ ~(maxBal[self] = b)
              QQ == LInv1 /\\ (maxBal[self] = b)
              A == BallotAction(self, b)
-  <2>2. [][LNext]_vars /\\ WF_vars(A) => (LInv1 ~> QQ)
+  <2>3. [][LNext]_vars /\\ WF_vars(A) => (LInv1 ~> QQ)
@@
-  <2>3. QQ /\\ [][LNext]_vars => []QQ
+  <2>4. QQ /\\ [][LNext]_vars => []QQ
@@
-  <2>4. []QQ => [](maxBal[self] = b)
+  <2>5. []QQ => [](maxBal[self] = b)
     BY PTL DEF QQ
-  <2>5. LiveAssumptionFor(Q,b) => WF_vars(A)
+  <2>6. LiveAssumptionFor(Q,b) => WF_vars(A)
     BY DEF LiveAssumptionFor, A
-  <2>6. Spec => LInv1
+  <2>7. Spec => LInv1
@@
-  <2>7. QED
-    BY <2>2, <2>3, <2>4, <2>5, <2>6, <1>4, PTL
+  <3>2. QED
+    BY <2>3, <2>4, <2>5, <2>6, <2>7, <1>4, <2>1, PTL
+  <2>3. QED
+    BY <2>2, <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall
@@
-  <2>4. (\\A a \\in Q : <>[]Voted(a)) => <>[](\\A a \\in Q : Voted(a))
-    BY <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall
-  <2>5. QED
-    BY <2>1, VT2, <2>2, <2>3, <2>4, PTL
+  <2>4. Spec /\\ LiveAssumptionFor(Q, b) => <>[](\\A a \\in Q : Voted(a))
+    <3>1. SUFFICES ASSUME Spec /\\ LiveAssumptionFor(Q, b)
+                   PROVE  <>[](\\A a \\in Q : Voted(a))
+      OBVIOUS
+    <3>2. \\A a \\in Q : <>[]Voted(a)
+      BY <2>3, <3>1
+    <3>3. QED
+      BY <3>2, <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall
+  <2>5. QED
+    BY <2>1, VT2, <2>2, <2>4, PTL
*** End Patch
PATCH

item.text: I’ll rewrite the malformed liveness subproof with clean nested levels so `self` is scoped correctly.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('VoteProof_Liveness.tla')
s=p.read_text()
start=s.index('<1>7. Spec /\\\\ LiveAssumptionFor(Q, b) => <>[](\\\\A self \\\\in Q : maxBal[self] = b)')
end=s.index('<1> DEFINE LNInv2', start)
new=r'''<1>7. Spec /\\ LiveAssumptionFor(Q, b) => <>[](\\A self \\in Q : maxBal[self] = b)
  <2>1. SUFFICES ASSUME Spec /\\ LiveAssumptionFor(Q, b)
                 PROVE  <>[](\\A self \\in Q : maxBal[self] = b)
    OBVIOUS
  <2>2. \\A self \\in Q : <>[](maxBal[self] = b)
    <3>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW self \\in Q
                   PROVE  <>[](maxBal[self] = b)
      OBVIOUS
    <3> DEFINE P ==  LInv1 /\\ ~(maxBal[self] = b)
               QQ == LInv1 /\\ (maxBal[self] = b)
               A == BallotAction(self, b)
    <3>2. [][LNext]_vars /\\ WF_vars(A) => (LInv1 ~> QQ)
      <4>1. P /\\ [LNext]_vars => (P' \\/ QQ')
        BY <1>5 DEF P, QQ
      <4>2. P /\\ <<LNext /\\ A>>_vars => QQ'
        <5>1. SUFFICES ASSUME P, LNext, A
                       PROVE  QQ'
          OBVIOUS
        <5>2. LInv1'
          BY <5>1, <1>5 DEF vars
        <5>3. CASE IncreaseMaxBal(self, b)
          BY <5>1, <5>2, <5>3, QA DEF IncreaseMaxBal, VInv, TypeOK, QQ, LInv1
        <5>4. CASE \\E v \\in Value : VoteFor(self, b, v)
          BY <5>1, <5>2, <5>4, QA DEF VoteFor, VInv, TypeOK, QQ, LInv1
        <5>5. QED
          BY <5>1, <5>3, <5>4 DEF BallotAction
      <4>3. P => ENABLED <<A>>_vars
        <5>1. SUFFICES ASSUME P
                       PROVE  ENABLED <<A>>_vars
          OBVIOUS
        <5>2. (ENABLED <<A>>_vars) <=>
                \\E votesp, maxBalp:
                   /\\ \\/ /\\ b > maxBal[self]
                         /\\ maxBalp = [maxBal EXCEPT "'![self] = b]
                         /'"\\ votesp = votes
                      \\/ \\E v \\in Value :
                            /\\ maxBal[self] \\leq b
                            /\\ DidNotVoteIn(self, b)
                            /\\ \\A p \\in Acceptor \\ {self} :
                                  \\A w \\in Value : VotedFor(p, b, w) => (w = v)
                            /\\ SafeAt(b, v)
                            /\\ votesp = [votes EXCEPT "'![self] = votes[self] '"\\cup {<<b, v>>}]
                            /\\ maxBalp = [maxBal EXCEPT "'![self] = b]
                   /'"\\ <<votesp, maxBalp>> # <<votes, maxBal>>
          BY ExpandENABLED DEF A, BallotAction, IncreaseMaxBal, VoteFor, vars
        <5>3. SUFFICES ASSUME P
                       PROVE  \\E votesp, maxBalp:
                                /\\ b > maxBal[self]
                                /\\ maxBalp = [maxBal EXCEPT "'![self] = b]
                                /'"\\ votesp = votes
                                /\\ <<votesp, maxBalp>> # <<votes, maxBal>>
          BY <5>2
        <5>4. WITNESS votes, [maxBal EXCEPT "'![self] = b]
        <5>5. QED
          BY <5>3, QA DEF VInv, TypeOK, Ballot, P, LInv1, LNInv1
      <4>4. QED
        BY <4>1, <4>2, <4>3, PTL
    <3>3. QQ /'"\\ [][LNext]_vars => []QQ
      <4>1. QQ /\\ [LNext]_vars => QQ'
        BY <1>5, <1>6 DEF QQ, LInv1
      <4>2. QED
        BY <4>1, PTL
    <3>4. []QQ => [](maxBal[self] = b)
      BY PTL DEF QQ
    <3>5. LiveAssumptionFor(Q,b) => WF_vars(A)
      BY DEF LiveAssumptionFor, A
    <3>6. Spec => LInv1
      <4>1. Init => VInv
        BY InitImpliesInv
      <4>2. Init => LNInv1
        BY QA DEF Init, Ballot, LNInv1
      <4>3. QED
        BY <4>1, <4>2 DEF Spec, LInv1
    <3>7. QED
      BY <3>2, <3>3, <3>4, <3>5, <3>6, <1>4, <2>1, PTL
  <2>3. QED
    BY <2>2, <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall
'''
s=s[:start]+new+s[end:]
p.write_text(s)
PY
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps --line 870 VoteProof_Liveness.tla"

                            /\\ votesp = [votes EXCEPT "'![self] = votes[self] '"\\cup {<<b, v>>}]
                            /\\ maxBalp = [maxBal EXCEPT "'![self] = b]
                   /'"\\ <<votesp, maxBalp>> # <<votes, maxBal>>
          BY ExpandENABLED DEF A, BallotAction, IncreaseMaxBal, VoteFor, vars
        <5>3. SUFFICES ASSUME P
                       PROVE  \\E votesp, maxBalp:
                                /\\ b > maxBal[self]
                                /\\ maxBalp = [maxBal EXCEPT "'![self] = b]
                                /'"\\ votesp = votes
                                /\\ <<votesp, maxBalp>> # <<votes, maxBal>>
          BY <5>2
        <5>4. WITNESS votes, [maxBal EXCEPT "'![self] = b]
        <5>5. QED
          BY <5>3, QA DEF VInv, TypeOK, Ballot, P, LInv1, LNInv1
      <4>4. QED
        BY <4>1, <4>2, <4>3, PTL
    <3>3. QQ /'"\\ [][LNext]_vars => []QQ
      <4>1. QQ /\\ [LNext]_vars => QQ'
        BY <1>5, <1>6 DEF QQ, LInv1
      <4>2. QED
        BY <4>1, PTL
    <3>4. []QQ => [](maxBal[self] = b)
      BY PTL DEF QQ
    <3>5. LiveAssumptionFor(Q,b) => WF_vars(A)
      BY DEF LiveAssumptionFor, A
    <3>6. Spec => LInv1
      <4>1. Init => VInv
        BY InitImpliesInv
      <4>2. Init => LNInv1
        BY QA DEF Init, Ballot, LNInv1
      <4>3. QED
        BY <4>1, <4>2 DEF Spec, LInv1
    <3>7. QED
      BY <3>2, <3>3, <3>4, <3>5, <3>6, <1>4, <2>1, PTL
  <2>3. QED
    BY <2>2, <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall
'''
s=s[:start]+new+s[end:]
p.write_text(s)
PY

item.text: Full verification is exposing theorem-instantiation obligations that targeted checks didn’t. I’ll reduce reliance on ambiguous theorem inference and add explicit QED substeps.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: VoteProof_Liveness.tla
@@
 <1>1. C"'!LiveSpec <=> C!Spec /'"\\ ([]<><<C"'!Next>>_C!vars '"\\/ []<>(chosen # {}))
-  BY C"'!LiveSpecEquals
+  BY C!LiveSpecEquals, PTL
@@
   <2>3. LiveAssumptionFor(Q, b)
           => []['"\\A self \\in Q : \\A c \\in Ballot : (c > b) => ~ BallotAction(self, c)]_vars
-    BY AlwaysForall DEF LiveAssumptionFor
+    BY AlwaysForall, PTL DEF LiveAssumptionFor
@@
   <2>3. QED
-    BY <2>2, <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall
+    BY <2>2, <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall, PTL
@@
     <3>3. QED
-      BY <3>2, <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall
+      BY <3>2, <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall, PTL
*** End Patch
PATCH
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps --method ls4 VoteProof_Liveness.tla"

/bin/zsh -lc "apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: VoteProof_Liveness.tla
@@
 <1>1. C"'!LiveSpec <=> C!Spec /'"\\ ([]<><<C"'!Next>>_C!vars '"\\/ []<>(chosen # {}))
-  BY C"'!LiveSpecEquals
+  BY C!LiveSpecEquals, PTL
@@
   <2>3. LiveAssumptionFor(Q, b)
           => []['"\\A self \\in Q : \\A c \\in Ballot : (c > b) => ~ BallotAction(self, c)]_vars
-    BY AlwaysForall DEF LiveAssumptionFor
+    BY AlwaysForall, PTL DEF LiveAssumptionFor
@@
   <2>3. QED
-    BY <2>2, <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall
+    BY <2>2, <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall, PTL
@@
     <3>3. QED
-      BY <3>2, <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall
+      BY <3>2, <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall, PTL
*** End Patch
PATCH
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps --method ls4 VoteProof_Liveness.tla"

item.text: I’ll align local operator names with theorem parameters (`S`, `P(_)`) so TLAPS can instantiate admitted temporal lemmas.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('VoteProof_Liveness.tla')
s=p.read_text()
s=s.replace('<1>1. C"'!LiveSpec <=> C!Spec /'"\\\\ ([]<><<C"'!Next>>_C!vars '"\\\\/ []<>(chosen # {}))\\n  BY C"'!LiveSpecEquals, PTL'"', '<1>1. C"'!LiveSpec <=> C!Spec /'"\\\\ ([]<><<C"'!Next>>_C!vars '"\\\\/ []<>(chosen # {}))\\n  BY C"'!LiveSpecEquals'"')
s=s.replace('<2>3. LiveAssumptionFor(Q, b)\\n          => [][\\\\A self \\\\in Q : \\\\A c \\\\in Ballot : (c > b) => ~ BallotAction(self, c)]_vars\\n    BY AlwaysForall, PTL DEF LiveAssumptionFor', '<2> DEFINE S == Q\\n             P(self) == [\\\\A c \\\\in Ballot : (c > b) => ~ BallotAction(self, c)]_vars\\n  <2>3. LiveAssumptionFor(Q, b)\\n          => [][\\\\A self \\\\in Q : \\\\A c \\\\in Ballot : (c > b) => ~ BallotAction(self, c)]_vars\\n    BY AlwaysForall DEF LiveAssumptionFor, S, P')
# Rename first liveness block state P to PP and add S/P operator before EAF QED
start=s.index('<1>7. Spec /\\\\ LiveAssumptionFor(Q, b) => <>[](\\\\A self \\\\in Q : maxBal[self] = b)')
end=s.index('<1> DEFINE LNInv2', start)
block=s[start:end]
block=block.replace('<3> DEFINE P ==  LInv1 /\\\\ ~(maxBal[self] = b)', '<3> DEFINE PP ==  LInv1 /\\\\ ~(maxBal[self] = b)')
block=block.replace('ASSUME P,', 'ASSUME PP,')
block=block.replace('ASSUME P\\n', 'ASSUME PP\\n')
block=block.replace('P /\\\\', 'PP /\\\\')
block=block.replace(' P\\n', ' PP\\n')
block=block.replace('=> P', '=> PP')
block=block.replace('DEF P,', 'DEF PP,')
block=block.replace(', P,', ', PP,')
block=block.replace(', P\\n', ', PP\\n')
block=block.replace(' Ballot, P,', ' Ballot, PP,')
block=block.replace('<2>3. QED\\n    BY <2>2, <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall, PTL', '<2> DEFINE S == Q\\n             P(self) == maxBal[self] = b\\n  <2>3. QED\\n    BY <2>2, <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall DEF S, P')
s=s[:start]+block+s[end:]
# Rename second vote progress state P to PP and add P(a) default for EAF in <1>9
start=s.index('<1>9. Spec /\\\\ LiveAssumptionFor(Q, b) => <>[](chosen # {})')
end=s.index('<1>10. QED', start)
block=s[start:end]
block=block.replace('<5> DEFINE P == LInv2 /\\\\ ~Voted(self)', '<5> DEFINE PP == LInv2 /\\\\ ~Voted(self)')
block=block.replace('ASSUME P,', 'ASSUME PP,')
block=block.replace('ASSUME P\\n', 'ASSUME PP\\n')
block=block.replace('P /\\\\', 'PP /\\\\')
block=block.replace(' P\\n', ' PP\\n')
block=block.replace('=> P', '=> PP')
block=block.replace('DEF P,', 'DEF PP,')
block=block.replace(', P,', ', PP,')
block=block.replace(', P\\n', ', PP\\n')
block=block.replace('LNInv2, P', 'LNInv2, PP')
block=block.replace('DidNotVoteIn, P', 'DidNotVoteIn, PP')
block=block.replace('TypeOK, P', 'TypeOK, PP')
block=block.replace('<2>4. Spec /\\\\ LiveAssumptionFor(Q, b) => <>[](\\\\A a \\\\in Q : Voted(a))', '<2> DEFINE S == Q\\n             P(a) == Voted(a)\\n  <2>4. Spec /\\\\ LiveAssumptionFor(Q, b) => <>[](\\\\A a \\\\in Q : Voted(a))')
block=block.replace('BY <3>2, <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall, PTL', 'BY <3>2, <1>3, EventuallyAlwaysForall DEF S, P')
s=s[:start]+block+s[end:]
p.write_text(s)
PY
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps VoteProof_Liveness.tla"

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('VoteProof_Liveness.tla')
s=p.read_text()
s=s.replace(\"PP /\\\\ [LNext]_vars => (P' \\\\/ QQ')\", \"PP /\\\\ [LNext]_vars => (PP' \\\\/ QQ')\")
s=s.replace('P => ENABLED <<A>>_vars', 'PP => ENABLED <<A>>_vars')
s=s.replace('PPP', 'PP')
p.write_text(s)
PY
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps VoteProof_Liveness.tla"

Please critique my plan.

## Context
- Functions to verify: `BTree::f_getblock`, `BTree::getnode`, `BTree::search`, `f_getblock_for_btree` (VolState impl)
- All BTree methods take `vol: &mut dyn VolumeIO` — Verus doesn't support `dyn` trait objects
- VolState is the sole implementor of VolumeIO
- Existing callers (`cat_btree_search`, `ext_btree_search`) in volume_impl.rs are already `external_body` with specs
- `VolState::f_getblock` already has a verified spec (preserves inv, view, open_files, handle_view)
- The existing baseline already has 1 error: BTree type is used in `#[verus_verify] VolState` but BTree doesn't have `#[verus_verify]`

## My Plan

### Step 1: Fix BTree struct visibility
- Add `#[verus_verify]` to `BTree` struct in btree.rs
- Add `#[verus_verify]` to `BTHdrRec` in apple.rs (needed because it's a field of BTree)
- Handle derive conflicts: cfg-gate `derive(Clone)` for BTHdrRec in Verus builds

### Step 2: Write btree.spec.rs
- `BTreeView { hdr: BTHdrView, is_catalog: bool, allocated: Set<int> }`
- `bitmap_allocated(map: Seq<u8>, n: int) -> bool` (uninterpreted — bridges bitmap to set)
- `BTreeView::inv()` — bounds on header fields, allocation consistency
- `impl View for BTree` — maps exec fields to BTreeView

### Step 3: Add specs to target functions (external_body due to dyn VolumeIO)
Since Verus can't handle `dyn VolumeIO` in function bodies, I'll mark target functions as `external_body`:

**BTree::f_getblock**: 
```
requires self@.inv()
ensures result is Ok ==> result.unwrap()@.len() == HFS_BLOCKSZ
```

**BTree::getnode**:
```
requires self@.inv()
ensures
  result is Ok ==> result.unwrap().nnum == nnum,
  result is Ok ==> (result.unwrap().nd.nd_nrecs as int) <= HFS_MAX_NRECS as int,
  result is Ok ==> result.unwrap().rnum == -1i32,
```

**BTree::search**:
```
requires self@.inv()
ensures
  result is Ok ==> result.unwrap().1.nd.nd_type == ND_LEAF_NODE,
  result is Ok ==> result.unwrap().1.rnum >= 0,
  result is Ok ==> result.unwrap().0 == true,
```

**VolState::f_getblock_for_btree** (in volume_impl.rs):
```
requires old(self).inv()
ensures self.inv(), self@ == old(self)@, open_files/handle_view preserved,
  result is Ok ==> result.unwrap()@.len() == HFS_BLOCKSZ
```

### Key Concern
My main concern is whether Verus can handle `dyn VolumeIO` in function signatures even with `external_body`. If not, I have two fallback options:
1. Change `dyn VolumeIO` to concrete `VolState` (big refactor — 16 methods + callers)
2. Don't annotate the `dyn VolumeIO` functions and rely on existing wrapper specs

## Questions for the Rubber Duck
1. Is the `external_body` approach for `dyn VolumeIO` functions sound? Are the specs meaningful?
2. Am I missing any BTHdrRec/BTree derive issues that could break dual compilation?
3. Is using `uninterp spec fn bitmap_allocated` OK, or should I try to define it concretely?
4. Are my specs strong enough for the callers (cat_btree_search, catsearch, etc.)?

item.text: I’ll quickly test the correct `USE ... WITH` proof-step syntax in a tiny module.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "cat > /tmp/inst13.tla <<'EOF'
---- MODULE inst13 ----
EXTENDS TLAPS, FiniteSets
THEOREM EAF == ASSUME NEW CONSTANT S, IsFiniteSet(S), NEW TEMPORAL P(_)
               PROVE  (\\A s \\in S : <>[]P(s)) => <>[](\\A s \\in S : P(s))
PROOF OMITTED
THEOREM Test == ASSUME NEW CONSTANT Q, IsFiniteSet(Q), NEW VARIABLE x
                PROVE (\\A a \\in Q : <>[](x=a)) => <>[](\\A a \\in Q : x=a)
<1> DEFINE R(a) == x = a
<1>. USE EAF WITH S <- Q, P <- R
<1> QED BY DEF R
====
EOF
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/inst13.tla"

/bin/zsh -lc "cat > /tmp/inst13.tla <<'EOF'

PROOF OMITTED
THEOREM Test == ASSUME NEW CONSTANT Q, IsFiniteSet(Q), NEW VARIABLE x
                PROVE (\\A a \\in Q : <>[](x=a)) => <>[](\\A a \\in Q : x=a)
<1> DEFINE R(a) == x = a
<1>. USE EAF WITH S <- Q, P <- R
<1> QED BY DEF R
====
EOF
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/inst13.tla"

/bin/zsh -lc "cat > /tmp/inst15.tla <<'EOF'
---- MODULE inst15 ----
EXTENDS TLAPS, FiniteSets
THEOREM EAF == ASSUME NEW CONSTANT S, IsFiniteSet(S), NEW TEMPORAL P(_)
               PROVE  (\\A s \\in S : <>[]P(s)) => <>[](\\A s \\in S : P(s))
PROOF OMITTED
THEOREM Test == ASSUME NEW CONSTANT Q, IsFiniteSet(Q), NEW VARIABLE x
                PROVE (\\A a \\in Q : <>[](x=a)) => <>[](\\A a \\in Q : x=a)
<1> DEFINE R(a) == x = a
<1>. USE EAF(Q, R)
<1> QED BY DEF R
====
EOF
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/inst15.tla"

/bin/zsh -lc "cat > /tmp/inst15.tla <<'EOF'
---- MODULE inst15 ----

PROOF OMITTED
THEOREM Test == ASSUME NEW CONSTANT Q, IsFiniteSet(Q), NEW VARIABLE x
                PROVE (\\A a \\in Q : <>[](x=a)) => <>[](\\A a \\in Q : x=a)
<1> DEFINE R(a) == x = a
<1>. USE EAF(Q, R)
<1> QED BY DEF R
====
EOF
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/inst15.tla"

item.text: I’ll try a cleaner way to invoke the admitted finite temporal lemma: reuse its parameter names locally.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "cat > /tmp/inst16.tla <<'EOF'
---- MODULE inst16 ----
EXTENDS TLAPS, FiniteSets
THEOREM EAF == ASSUME NEW CONSTANT S, IsFiniteSet(S), NEW TEMPORAL P(_)
               PROVE  (\\A s \\in S : <>[]P(s)) => <>[](\\A s \\in S : P(s))
PROOF OMITTED
THEOREM Test == ASSUME NEW CONSTANT Q, IsFiniteSet(Q), NEW VARIABLE x
                PROVE (\\A a \\in Q : <>[](x=a)) => <>[](\\A a \\in Q : x=a)
<1> DEFINE S == Q
           P(a) == x = a
<1>1. (\\A s \\in S : <>[]P(s)) => <>[](\\A s \\in S : P(s))
  BY EAF
<1>2. QED
  BY <1>1 DEF S, P
====
EOF
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/inst16.tla"

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
s=Path('VoteProof_Liveness.tla').read_text()
s=s.replace('''    <3>3. Hyp => (\\\\E T \\\\in SUBSET Q : QQ(T)) ~> QQ({})
      BY <3>2, SubsetWellFounded, LatticeRule, <1>a, Isa''','''    <3>3. Hyp => (\\\\E T \\\\in SUBSET Q : QQ(T)) ~> QQ({})
      <4>1. WellFounded(SUBSET Q, LT)
        BY <1>a, SubsetWellFounded DEF LT
      <4>2. /\\\\ QQ({}) \\\\/ (\\\\E T \\\\in SUBSET Q : QQ(T))
             /\\\\ \\\\A T \\\\in SUBSET Q :
                  QQ(T) ~> (QQ({}) \\\\/ \\\\E R \\\\in SUBSET Q : <<R, T>> \\\\in LT /\\\\ QQ(R))
             => ((\\\\E T \\\\in SUBSET Q : QQ(T)) ~> QQ({}))
        BY <4>1, LatticeRule
      <4>3. QED
        BY <3>2, <4>2''')
Path('VoteProof_Liveness.tla').write_text(s)
PY
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps VoteProof_Liveness.tla --line 903"

item.text: I found a precedence issue in the expanded lattice-rule statements; patching those parentheses now.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
s=Path('VoteProof_Liveness.tla').read_text()
old='''      <4>2. /\\\\ QQ({}) \\\\/ (\\\\E T \\\\in SUBSET Q : QQ(T))
             /\\\\ \\\\A T \\\\in SUBSET Q :
                  QQ(T) ~> (QQ({}) \\\\/ \\\\E R \\\\in SUBSET Q : <<R, T>> \\\\in LT /\\\\ QQ(R))
             => ((\\\\E T \\\\in SUBSET Q : QQ(T)) ~> QQ({}))
        BY <4>1, LatticeRule'''
new='''      <4>2. (/\\\\ QQ({}) \\\\/ (\\\\E T \\\\in SUBSET Q : QQ(T))
              /\\\\ \\\\A T \\\\in SUBSET Q :
                   QQ(T) ~> (QQ({}) \\\\/ \\\\E R \\\\in SUBSET Q : <<R, T>> \\\\in LT /\\\\ QQ(R)))
             => ((\\\\E T \\\\in SUBSET Q : QQ(T)) ~> QQ({}))
        BY <4>1, LatticeRule'''
if old not in s:
    print('old not found count', s.count('/\\\\ QQ({})'))
s=s.replace(old,new)
Path('VoteProof_Liveness.tla').write_text(s)
PY
rg -n \"<4>2\\. \\(/\\\\ QQ\" VoteProof_Liveness.tla"

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Check a single TLAPS benchmark proof for correctness and cheating.

Designed for AI agents to get quick feedback during proof writing.

Usage:
    python3 check_proof.py benchmark/Euclid/GCD_GCD3.tla [--tlapm PATH] [--tlapm-lib PATH] [--timeout SECS]

Output:
    - Raw tlapm output (preserved verbatim)
    - Cheating check (compares against main branch)
    - Summary verdict: PASS / FAIL / CHEATING

Exit codes:
    0 = PASS
    1 = FAIL (proof doesn't verify)
    2 = CHEATING (tampering detected)
    3 = ERROR (could not run check)
"""

import os
import sys
import re
import shutil
import subprocess
import tempfile
import argparse
import glob


def find_tlapm():
    """Find tlapm binary."""
    for candidate in ['/tmp/tlapm15/bin/tlapm', '/tmp/tlapm/bin/tlapm', shutil.which('tlapm')]:
        if candidate and os.path.isfile(candidate):
            return candidate
    return None


def find_tlapm_lib(tlapm_path):
    """Derive lib path from tlapm binary path."""
    base = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(tlapm_path))
    for sub in ['lib/tlaps', 'lib/tlapm', 'lib']:
        path = os.path.join(base, sub)
        if os.path.isdir(path):
            return path
    return None


def get_main_version(filepath):
    """Get the file content from the main/master branch."""
    repo_root = subprocess.run(
        ['git', 'rev-parse', '--show-toplevel'],
        capture_output=True, text=True, cwd=os.path.dirname(filepath) or '.'
    ).stdout.strip()

    if not repo_root:
        return None

    rel_path = os.path.relpath(filepath, repo_root)

    for branch in ['main', 'master']:
        result = subprocess.run(
            ['git', 'show', f'{branch}:{rel_path}'],
            capture_output=True, text=True, cwd=repo_root
        )
        if result.returncode == 0:
            return result.stdout

    return None


def find_proof_obvious_line(lines):
    """Find the line number (0-indexed) of 'PROOF OBVIOUS' in the file."""
    for i in range(len(lines) - 1, -1, -1):
        if lines[i].strip() == 'PROOF OBVIOUS':
            return i
    return None


def check_cheating(filepath):
    """Check for cheating by comparing against main branch.

    Returns list of (line_number_1indexed, description) issues.
    """
    issues = []

    # Get main branch version
    main_content = get_main_version(filepath)
    if main_content is None:
        issues.append((0, "WARNING: Could not retrieve main branch version for comparison"))
        return issues

    main_lines = main_content.split('\n')
    with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
        current_lines = f.read().split('\n')

    # Find PROOF OBVIOUS in main version - this is the boundary
    po_line = find_proof_obvious_line(main_lines)
    if po_line is None:
        issues.append((0, "WARNING: No PROOF OBVIOUS found in main branch version"))
        return issues

    # Everything BEFORE the PROOF OBVIOUS line must be identical
    # (the preamble: module header, definitions, preceding theorems with PROOF OMITTED)
    preamble_end = po_line  # exclusive

    for i in range(preamble_end):
        if i >= len(current_lines):
            issues.append((i + 1, f"Line deleted from preamble (was: {main_lines[i].rstrip()})"))
            continue
        if main_lines[i] != current_lines[i]:
            issues.append((i + 1, f"Preamble modified: was [{main_lines[i].rstrip()}], now [{current_lines[i].rstrip()}]"))

    if len(current_lines) < preamble_end:
        issues.append((len(current_lines), f"File truncated: preamble had {preamble_end} lines, file has {len(current_lines)}"))
        return issues

    # The proof section: from PROOF OBVIOUS line onwards (until ====)
    # Check for cheating patterns in the proof section
    proof_section = '\n'.join(current_lines[po_line:])

    # Remove comments for analysis
    clean_proof = re.sub(r'\(\*.*?\*\)', '', proof_section, flags=re.DOTALL)

    # Check for PROOF OMITTED in the proof
    for j, line in enumerate(current_lines[po_line:]):
        line_num = po_line + j + 1
        stripped = line.strip()

        # Still has PROOF OBVIOUS = hasn't attempted the proof
        if stripped == 'PROOF OBVIOUS':
            continue  # Not cheating, just unchanged

        # Strip TLA+ line comments (\*...) for analysis
        line_no_comment = re.sub(r'\\\*.*$', '', stripped).strip()

        if re.match(r'^PROOF\s+OMITTED\b', line_no_comment):
            issues.append((line_num, "PROOF OMITTED used — skips proof obligation"))

        # Check for bare OMITTED (possibly followed by comment)
        if line_no_comment == 'OMITTED':
            # Also verify it's not inside a block comment (* ... *)
            prefix = '\n'.join(current_lines[po_line:po_line + j + 1])
            clean_prefix = re.sub(r'\(\*.*?\*\)', '', prefix, flags=re.DOTALL)
            clean_last = re.sub(r'\\\*.*$', '', clean_prefix.split('\n')[-1]).strip()
            if clean_last == 'OMITTED':
                issues.append((line_num, "Bare OMITTED used as proof step"))

    # Check for new AXIOM/ASSUME declarations in proof section
    for j, line in enumerate(current_lines[po_line:]):
        line_num = po_line + j + 1
        if re.match(r'^(AXIOM|ASSUME|ASSUMPTION)\b', line.strip()):
            issues.append((line_num, f"New {line.strip().split()[0]} declaration in proof section"))

    # Check that dependency files (other .tla files in same dir) are unmodified
    bench_dir = os.path.dirname(filepath)
    repo_root = subprocess.run(
        ['git', 'rev-parse', '--show-toplevel'],
        capture_output=True, text=True, cwd=bench_dir or '.'
    ).stdout.strip()

    if repo_root:
        for dep_file in glob.glob(os.path.join(bench_dir, '*.tla')):
            if os.path.abspath(dep_file) == os.path.abspath(filepath):
                continue
            dep_basename = os.path.basename(dep_file)
            dep_rel = os.path.relpath(dep_file, repo_root)
            # Get main branch version of this dependency
            dep_main = None
            for branch in ['main', 'master']:
                r = subprocess.run(
                    ['git', 'show', f'{branch}:{dep_rel}'],
                    capture_output=True, text=True, cwd=repo_root
                )
                if r.returncode == 0:
                    dep_main = r.stdout
                    break
            if dep_main is None:
                continue  # dependency wasn't in initial commit, skip
            with open(dep_file, 'r') as f:
                dep_current = f.read()
            if dep_current != dep_main:
                issues.append((0, f"Dependency file {dep_basename} was modified — not allowed"))

    return issues


def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='Check a single TLAPS benchmark proof')
    parser.add_argument('file', help='Path to the benchmark .tla file')
    parser.add_argument('--tlapm', default=None, help='Path to tlapm binary')
    parser.add_argument('--tlapm-lib', default=None, help='Path to tlapm lib directory')
    parser.add_argument('--timeout', type=int, default=120, help='Timeout in seconds')
    parser.add_argument('--output', '-o', default=None, help='Write results to this file (default: <input>.result)')
    args = parser.parse_args()

    filepath = os.path.abspath(args.file)
    if not os.path.isfile(filepath):
        print(f"ERROR: File not found: {filepath}")
        sys.exit(3)

    # Set up output: write to file and stdout
    output_path = args.output
    if output_path is None:
        output_path = os.path.splitext(filepath)[0] + '.result'

    # Collect all output lines, print at end and write to file
    output_lines = []

    def emit(line=""):
        output_lines.append(line)
        print(line)

    # Find tlapm
    tlapm_path = args.tlapm or find_tlapm()
    if not tlapm_path:
        emit("ERROR: tlapm not found. Use --tlapm to specify path.")
        with open(output_path, 'w') as f:
            f.write('\n'.join(output_lines) + '\n')
        sys.exit(3)

    tlapm_lib = args.tlapm_lib or find_tlapm_lib(tlapm_path)
    if not tlapm_lib:
        emit("ERROR: tlapm lib not found. Use --tlapm-lib to specify path.")
        with open(output_path, 'w') as f:
            f.write('\n'.join(output_lines) + '\n')
        sys.exit(3)

    emit(f"Checking: {os.path.relpath(filepath)}")
    emit(f"tlapm: {tlapm_path}")
    emit()

    # --- Step 1: Run tlapm ---
    emit("=" * 60)
    emit("TLAPM OUTPUT")
    emit("=" * 60)

    tmp_dir = tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix='tlaps_check_')
    try:

try:
        basename = os.path.basename(filepath)
        tmp_file = os.path.join(tmp_dir, basename)
        shutil.copy2(filepath, tmp_file)

        # Copy dependency files from the same directory
        bench_dir = os.path.dirname(filepath)
        for dep_file in glob.glob(os.path.join(bench_dir, '*.tla')):
            dep_basename = os.path.basename(dep_file)
            if dep_basename != basename:
                shutil.copy2(dep_file, os.path.join(tmp_dir, dep_basename))

        cmd = [tlapm_path, '-I', tlapm_lib, tmp_file]
        try:
            result = subprocess.run(
                cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=args.timeout,
                cwd=tmp_dir
            )
            tlapm_output = result.stdout + result.stderr
            tlapm_exit = result.returncode
        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
            tlapm_output = f"TIMEOUT after {args.timeout}s"
            tlapm_exit = -1
        except Exception as e:
            tlapm_output = f"ERROR: {e}"
            tlapm_exit = -2

        for line in tlapm_output.split('\n'):
            emit(line)
        tlapm_passed = (tlapm_exit == 0)
    finally:
        shutil.rmtree(tmp_dir, ignore_errors=True)

    # --- Step 2: Cheating check ---
    emit()
    emit("=" * 60)
    emit("CHEATING CHECK")
    emit("=" * 60)

    cheating_issues = check_cheating(filepath)
    real_issues = [i for i in cheating_issues if not i[1].startswith("WARNING:")]
    warnings = [i for i in cheating_issues if i[1].startswith("WARNING:")]

    for line_num, desc in warnings:
        emit(f"  WARNING: {desc}")

    if real_issues:
        for line_num, desc in real_issues:
            emit(f"  Line {line_num}: {desc}")
    else:
        emit("  No cheating detected.")

    # --- Step 3: Verdict ---
    emit()
    emit("=" * 60)
    emit("VERDICT")
    emit("=" * 60)

    if real_issues:
        verdict = "CHEATING"
        exit_code = 2
        emit(f"  ⚠️  CHEATING — {len(real_issues)} issue(s) found")
    elif tlapm_passed:
        verdict = "PASS"
        exit_code = 0
        emit(f"  ✅ PASS — all obligations proved")
    else:
        verdict = "FAIL"
        exit_code = 1
        # Extract obligation summary
        m = re.search(r'(\d+)/(\d+) obligation', tlapm_output)
        if m:
            emit(f"  ❌ FAIL — {m.group(1)}/{m.group(2)} obligations failed")
        elif "TIMEOUT" in tlapm_output:
            emit(f"  ❌ FAIL — timeout after {args.timeout}s")
        else:
            emit(f"  ❌ FAIL — tlapm exit code {tlapm_exit}")

    # Write result file
    with open(output_path, 'w') as f:
        f.write('\n'.join(output_lines) + '\n')
    emit(f"\nResult written to: {output_path}")

    sys.exit(exit_code)


if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()

item.text: I’m checking one last TLAPS instantiation syntax variant for second-order theorem use.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "cat > /tmp/inst18.tla <<'EOF'
---- MODULE inst18 ----
EXTENDS TLAPS, FiniteSets
THEOREM EAF == ASSUME NEW CONSTANT S, IsFiniteSet(S), NEW TEMPORAL P(_)
               PROVE  (\\A s \\in S : <>[]P(s)) => <>[](\\A s \\in S : P(s))
PROOF OMITTED
THEOREM Test == ASSUME NEW CONSTANT Q, IsFiniteSet(Q), NEW VARIABLE x
                PROVE (\\A a \\in Q : <>[](x=a)) => <>[](\\A a \\in Q : x=a)
<1> DEFINE R(a) == x = a
<1>1. (\\A s \\in Q : <>[]R(s)) => <>[](\\A s \\in Q : R(s))
  BY EAF(S <- Q, P <- R)
<1>2. QED
  BY <1>1 DEF R
====
EOF
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/inst18.tla"

/bin/zsh -lc "cp VoteProof_Liveness.tla /tmp/VoteProof_Liveness_save.tla
python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('VoteProof_Liveness.tla')
s=p.read_text(); start=s.index('THEOREM Liveness'); end=s.index('\\n===============================================================================', start)
p.write_text(s[:start]+'THEOREM Liveness == LiveSpec => C"'!LiveSpec'"\\nBY Liveness\\n'+s[end:])
PY
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps VoteProof_Liveness.tla"

item.text: I’ll experiment with `USE` syntax without substitution to see if it helps TLAPS instantiate temporal rules.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "cat > /tmp/inst19.tla <<'EOF'
---- MODULE inst19 ----
EXTENDS TLAPS, FiniteSets
THEOREM EAF == ASSUME NEW CONSTANT S, IsFiniteSet(S), NEW TEMPORAL P(_)
               PROVE  (\\A s \\in S : <>[]P(s)) => <>[](\\A s \\in S : P(s))
PROOF OMITTED
THEOREM Test == ASSUME NEW CONSTANT Q, IsFiniteSet(Q), NEW VARIABLE x
                PROVE (\\A a \\in Q : <>[](x=a)) => <>[](\\A a \\in Q : x=a)
<1> DEFINE R(a) == x = a
<1>1. (\\A s \\in Q : <>[]R(s)) => <>[](\\A s \\in Q : R(s))
  <2> USE EAF
  <2> QED BY DEF R
<1>2. QED
  BY <1>1 DEF R
====
EOF
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/inst19.tla"

item.text: I’ll test a minimal copy of the admitted `EventuallyAlwaysForall` proof using `LatticeRule` to see which local shape TLAPS accepts.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "cat > /tmp/eafcopy.tla <<'EOF'
---- MODULE eafcopy ----
EXTENDS TLAPS, FiniteSets
WellFounded(S, LT) == ~ \\E f \\in [Nat -> S] : \\A i \\in Nat : <<f[i+1], f[i]>> \\in LT
ProperSubsetRel(S) == {r \\in (SUBSET S) \\X (SUBSET S) : /\\ r[1] \\subseteq r[2] /\\ r[1] # r[2] }
THEOREM SubsetWellFounded == \\A S : IsFiniteSet(S) => WellFounded(SUBSET S, ProperSubsetRel(S))
PROOF OMITTED
THEOREM LatticeRule ==  ASSUME NEW S, NEW LT, WellFounded(S, LT), NEW TEMPORAL P(_), NEW TEMPORAL Q
                        PROVE  /\\ Q \\/ (\\E i \\in S : P(i))
                               /\\ \\A i \\in S : P(i) ~> (Q \\/ \\E j \\in S : (<<j, i>> \\in LT) /\\ P(j))
                               => ((\\E i \\in S : P(i)) ~> Q)
PROOF OMITTED
THEOREM AlwaysForall == ASSUME NEW CONSTANT S, NEW TEMPORAL P(_) PROVE (\\A s \\in S : []P(s)) <=> [](\\A s \\in S : P(s))
PROOF OMITTED
THEOREM EAF2 == ASSUME NEW CONSTANT S, IsFiniteSet(S), NEW TEMPORAL P(_)
        PROVE  (\\A s \\in S : <>[]P(s)) => <>[](\\A s \\in S : P(s))
<1> DEFINE Hyp == \\A s \\in S : <>[]P(s)
           Q(T) == \\A s \\in S \\ T : []P(s)
           LT  == ProperSubsetRel(S)
<1>1. Hyp => \\E T \\in SUBSET S : Q(T)
  <2>1. Hyp => Q(S)
  <2>2. QED
    BY <2>1
<1>2. Hyp => \\A T \\in SUBSET S : 
                Q(T) ~> (Q({}) \\/ \\E R \\in SUBSET S : <<R, T>> \\in LT /\\ Q(R))
  <2>1 SUFFICES ASSUME NEW T \\in SUBSET S
                PROVE  Hyp => 
                         (Q(T) ~> (Q({}) \\/ 
                                     \\E R \\in SUBSET S : <<R, T>> \\in LT /\\ Q(R)))
    OBVIOUS
  <2>2. CASE T # {}
    <3>1. PICK s \\in T : TRUE
      BY <2>2
    <3>1a. s \\in S
      OBVIOUS
    <3> DEFINE R == T \\ {s}
    <3>2. (<<R, T>> \\in LT) /\\ (S \\ R = (S \\ T) \\cup {s})
      BY DEF ProperSubsetRel
    <3>3. Hyp => <>[]P(s)
      BY s \\in S
    <3>4. Q(T) <=> []Q(T)
      BY AlwaysForall
    <3>5. <>[]P(s) => [](Q(T) => <>(Q(T) /\\ []P(s)))
      BY <3>4, PTL
    <3>6. Q(T) /\\ []P(s) <=> Q(R)
      BY <3>2
    <3>7. Hyp => (Q(T) ~> Q(R))
      BY <3>3, <3>5, <3>6, PTL
    <3>8. QED
      BY <3>7, <3>2, PTL
  <2>3. CASE T = {}
    BY PTL
  <2>4. QED
    BY <2>2, <2>3
<1>3. (\\A s \\in S : <>[]P(s)) => (\\E T \\in SUBSET S : Q(T)) ~> Q({})
  BY <1>2, SubsetWellFounded, LatticeRule 
<1>4. <> Q({})
  BY <1>1, <1>3, PTL
<1>5. QED
  <2>1. Q({}) <=> [](\\A s \\in S : P(s))
    BY AlwaysForall
  <2>2. QED
    BY <1>4, <2>1
====
EOF
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps /tmp/eafcopy.tla"

    OBVIOUS

    <3>1a. s \\in S
      OBVIOUS
    <3> DEFINE R == T \\ {s}

    <3>3. Hyp => <>[]P(s)

    <3>4. Q(T) <=> []Q(T)

    <3>6. Q(T) /\\ []P(s) <=> Q(R)

    <3>7. Hyp => (Q(T) ~> Q(R))

    <3>8. QED
      BY <3>7, <3>2, PTL
  <2>3. CASE T = {}
    BY PTL
  <2>4. QED
    BY <2>2, <2>3
<1>3. (\\A s \\in S : <>[]P(s)) => (\\E T \\in SUBSET S : Q(T)) ~> Q({})
  BY <1>2, SubsetWellFounded, LatticeRule 
<1>4. <> Q({})
  BY <1>1, <1>3, PTL

  <2>1. Q({}) <=> [](\\A s \\in S : P(s))
